# Foolproof Definition

A foolproof autonomous marketing system is not a system that guarantees every product sells.

It is a system that prevents the agent from:

- staying vague,
- skipping measurement,
- confusing attribution with causation,
- scaling bad economics,
- generating random copy after failure,
- losing learning between campaigns,
- treating one failed test as market truth,
- treating one lucky test as durable truth.

## Foolproof means

```text
Every campaign creates evidence.
Every failure becomes a diagnosis.
Every diagnosis becomes a better next test.
Every result updates memory.
Every scaling decision passes economics.
```
